reasoning: Rickettsialpox is a mild, rare rickettsial infection caused by Rickettsia akari and transmitted by mites. It is easily and effectively treated with doxycycline. Because the disease is rare, usually self-limiting, and has a highly effective antibiotic treatment, there is virtually zero public health or commercial incentive to develop a vaccine. Furthermore, vaccine development, even if it were underway, takes years or decades and would not abruptly conclude and be added to Wikipedia within a 1 to 3 month time horizon.
